questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the values.

**Working.**
$$10+20+30+40=100$$

**Answer.** $100$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $10, 20, 30, 40$ and $50$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum divided by count.

**Working.**
$$\frac{10+20+30+40+50}{5}=\frac{150}{5}=30$$

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $2, 4, 8, 16, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each term is double the previous (geometric, ratio $2$).

**Working.** $16\times 2 = 32$.

**Answer.** $32$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a code, 'ROCK' is written as 'QNBJ'. Using the same rule, 'SAND' is written as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Determine the shift: R→Q is $-1$.

**Working.** Each letter shifts back by one: S→R, A→Z, N→M, D→C gives RZMC.

**Answer.** RZMC.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $5000$ is divided among A, B and C in the ratio $2:3:5$. C's share is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share = total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** C's fraction $=\dfrac{5}{2+3+5}=\dfrac{5}{10}$, so $5000\times\dfrac{1}{2}=2500$.

**Answer.** $2500$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: No metals are gases. All mercury is metal. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combine a universal negative with a universal affirmative.

**Working.** Mercury ⊆ metals and metals ∩ gases $=\varnothing$, so no mercury is a gas.

**Answer.** No mercury is gas.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which word reads the same when viewed in a vertical mirror?

figure.caption: The word MOM with a dashed vertical mirror line to its right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='30' y='72' font-size='52' fill='#334155'>MOM</text><line x1='195' y1='12' x2='195' y2='98' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each letter must be vertically symmetric and the word a palindrome.

**Working.** M, O, M are all vertically symmetric and 'MOM' is a palindrome, so the mirror image is identical.

**Answer.** MOM.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: The stable, self-perpetuating community reached at the end of ecological succession is the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Succession culminates in a climax community in equilibrium with the environment.

**Working.**
Climax community.

**Answer.** Climax community

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A tannery's discharge requires $₹4.5$ lakh of effluent-treatment (abatement) and additionally causes $₹2.8$ lakh of measurable downstream crop damage. Under the polluter-pays principle, the amount (in $₹$ lakh) the tannery must internalise is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The polluter-pays principle internalises the full externality: the polluter bears abatement **plus** damage costs (not just one of them).

**Working.**
$$4.5+2.8=7.3\text{ lakh}.$$

**Answer.** $₹7.3$ lakh

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 1.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=3.75\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $3.75\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.231\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a reactor, $30\,\text{mg/L}$ of biomass is produced while $120\,\text{mg/L}$ of substrate is consumed. The observed yield $Y_{X/S}$ is _____ mg/mg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Y_{X/S}=\dfrac{\Delta X}{\Delta S}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{30}{120}=0.25.$$

**Answer.** $0.25$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste $1500\,\text{kg}$ yields $0.12\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$ gas. Total gas is _____ m³.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas = mass × yield.

**Working.**
$$1500\times0.12=180.$$

**Answer.** $180$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** t½ = ln2/k.

**Working.**
$$0.693/0.231=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{10\times1000}{400}=25.$$

**Answer.** $25$

questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $3$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$12000\times0.1^{2}=120.$$

**Answer.** $120$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">6</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$6+1.5=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $4$ identical sources each $80\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log4=86.02.$$

**Answer.** $86.02\,\text{dB}$

questions.stem: Which stage of the EIA cycle immediately follows screening?

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order: screening → scoping → impact prediction → EMP → review/clearance.

**Working.**
Scoping follows screening.

**Answer.** Scoping

$$2000(1-40/100)=1200.$$

PDCA.

questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=100\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 30 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 40 m,  σ_z = 15 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100}{\pi\times6\times40\times15}e^{-30^2/(2\times15^2)}=0.00119663.$$

**Answer.** $11.966\times10^{-4}$

**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 3</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Present worth $=\dfrac{F}{(1+i)^n}$, with $F$ and $n$ read from the cash-flow diagram.

$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.1)^{3}}=37566.$$

**Answer.** $₹37566$

**Answer.** $274.1$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 20</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times100}{20+100}=0.25.$$

**Answer.** $0.25$
